core_model.structure_summary: Minimization of a composite objective over learned vector representations of graph nodes, combining a log-sigmoid contrastive term with negative sampling and squared-error reconstruction terms, with representations produced by normalized bilinear-attention maps over random-walk statistics.

variables.meaning: learned vector representations (embeddings) of graph nodes
composite loss: log-sigmoid contrastive term with negative sampling plus squared-error reconstruction regularizers
attention/MLP weight matrices producing embeddings from random-walk statistics
co-occurrence weights p_ij and negative-sampling counts n_j from random walks on the graph
temperature tau, negative-sampling factor Q, and mixing weight alpha

notes: Central object is a learned-embedding optimization: a skip-gram-style contrastive (log-sigmoid + negative sampling) loss jointly with reconstruction regularizers, minimized over node representations. Inputs are random-walk statistics on graph topology (hence stochastic sampling and network structure), and the encoder uses scaled dot-product / re-attention maps with softmax normalization. The paper also derives the stationary condition z_ij = ln p_ij - ln(Q n_j) as a fixed point of the loss gradient. Several parallel loss components make the single-core-model choice somewhat ambiguous; I take the overall joint-optimization objective as the core.
